parameters.query:
UPDATE document_sequences
SET last_number = last_number + 1,
    updated_at  = NOW()
WHERE document_type = 'invoice'
  AND year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM NOW())::int
RETURNING
  prefix || '-' || year::text || '-' || LPAD(last_number::text, 3, '0') AS document_number,
  last_number AS invoice_seq;

SELECT
  id::text                                          AS customer_uuid,
  COALESCE(company_name, full_name, name, '')       AS client_company_name,
  COALESCE(contact_name, full_name, '')             AS client_contact_name,
  COALESCE(street, address_street, '')              AS client_street,
  COALESCE(zip, postal_code, '')                    AS client_zip,
  COALESCE(city, '')                                AS client_city,
  COALESCE(country, 'Deutschland')                  AS client_country,
  COALESCE(vat_id, '')                              AS client_vat_id,
  COALESCE(customer_number::text, id::text)         AS client_number
FROM customers
WHERE id = '{{customer_id}}'::uuid
LIMIT 1;

INSERT INTO documents (
  document_number,
  document_type,
  title,
  language,
  status,
  customer_id,
  lead_id,
  amount_net,
  amount_tax,
  amount_gross,
  tax_rate,
  currency,
  file_format,
  mime_type,
  generated_by,
  tags,
  notes
)
VALUES (
  '{{document_number}}',
  'invoice',
  'Rechnung {{document_number}}',
  '{{language}}',
  'draft',
  '{{customer_id}}'::uuid,
  NULLIF('{{lead_id}}', '')::uuid,
  '{{amount_net}}'::numeric,
  '{{amount_tax}}'::numeric,
  '{{amount_gross}}'::numeric,
  '{{tax_rate}}'::numeric,
  'EUR',
  'pdf',
  'application/pdf',
  'GENERATE-invoice',
  jsonb_build_object(
    'template_key',    'invoice_template',
    'service_period',  '{{service_period}}',
    'due_date',        '{{due_date}}',
    'subject',         '{{subject}}',
    'tax_note',        '{{tax_note}}'
  ),
  NULLIF('{{notes}}', '')
)
RETURNING
  id::text     AS document_id,
  document_number,
  status,
  amount_gross AS total;

fields.name: next_step
fields.value: POST document_id + all variables to PDF renderer → renderer updates documents record with file_url, storage_key

parameters.noteText: GENERATE-invoice — Creates a numbered invoice record REQUIRED INPUTS: customer_id UUID ID from customers table language TEXT de / en service_period TEXT e.g. "Juni 2026" (§14 UStG: Leistungszeitraum) due_date TEXT e.g. "2026-06-22" subject TEXT e.g. "CrowPus Plattform — Monatliches Paket" invoice_rows TEXT HTML rows for line items (see format below) subtotal_net TEXT e.g. "299,00 €" tax_rate TEXT e.g. "19" (percent as number) tax_amount TEXT e.g. "56,81 €" total_gross TEXT e.g. "355,81 €" amount_net NUMBER 299.00 (numeric, for DB record) amount_tax NUMBER 56.81 amount_gross NUMBER 355.81 OPTIONAL INPUTS (leave "" to skip): lead_id UUID if billing a lead before conversion tax_note TEXT e.g. "Steuerschuldnerschaft des Leistungsempfängers §13b UStG" notes TEXT internal notes invoice_rows FORMAT (HTML): 1 CrowPus Plattform — Monatliches Paket 1 Monat 299,00 € 299,00 € OUTPUT: document_number DEE-INV-2026-001 document_id UUID of the documents record status draft

GENERATE-invoice — 4-step pipeline

Step 1 — DB: Get invoice number
  UPDATE document_sequences (atomic, concurrent-safe)
  → document_number = DEE-INV-2026-001

Step 2 — DB: Get customer details
  SELECT from customers WHERE id = {{customer_id}}
  → client_company_name, client_contact_name,
    client_street, client_zip, client_city,
    client_country, client_vat_id, client_number

Step 3 — DB: Create draft document record
  INSERT INTO documents (document_number, amounts, tags...)
  tags JSONB stores: template_key, service_period, due_date,
                     subject, tax_note
  → document_id, document_number, status='draft'

Step 4 — SET: Output all data needed for PDF renderer
  Returns everything the PDF service needs to:
    a) Fetch template from document_templates
    b) Substitute all {{variables}} in the HTML
    c) Generate PDF via Puppeteer
    d) Upload to GCS: invoices/{{year}}/{{document_number}}.pdf
    e) UPDATE documents SET file_url, storage_key... WHERE id = document_id

── WHEN PDF SERVICE IS READY ─────────────────────────────
Add between Step 3 and Step 4:
  HTTP node → PDF generation service
    POST /generate-pdf
    {
      "document_id":    "{{document_id}}",
      "document_number":"{{document_number}}",
      "template_key":   "invoice_template",
      "language":       "{{language}}",
      "variables": {
        "invoice_number":     "{{document_number}}",
        "invoice_date":       "{{invoice_date}}",
        "service_period":     "{{service_period}}",
        "due_date":           "{{due_date}}",
        "client_company_name":"{{client_company_name}}",
        ... all template vars
      }
    }

invoice_template — all {{variables}} needed for HTML render

Document info:
  invoice_number        DEE-INV-2026-001
  invoice_date          08.06.2026
  service_period        Juni 2026
  due_date              22.06.2026

Client info (from customers table):
  client_number         from customers.customer_number
  client_company_name   from customers.company_name
  client_contact_name   from customers.contact_name
  client_street         from customers.street
  client_zip            from customers.zip
  client_city           from customers.city
  client_country        from customers.country
  client_vat_id         from customers.vat_id

Line items (HTML):
  invoice_rows          <tr>...</tr> HTML rows
  subject               invoice description line

Financials:
  subtotal_net          "299,00 €"
  tax_rate              "19"
  tax_amount            "56,81 €"
  total_gross           "355,81 €"
  tax_note              "" or "§13b UStG" etc.

Storage path after upload:
  storage_key    invoices/2026/DEE-INV-2026-001.pdf
  storage_bucket crowpus-docs-bucket
  file_url       signed GCS URL
